articles.title: OPENAI PREPARES TO LAUNCH GPT-5 IN AUGUST
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to launch GPT-5 in early August, offering integrated o3 reasoning capabilities and mini/nano versions available through its API. GPT-5 aims to unify OpenAI's language models and simplify usage across applications, although it won't reach AGI status immediately. The anticipated declaration of AGI could impact OpenAI's revenue agreement with Microsoft.

articles.title: CROWDSTRIKE-BACKED COMPLIANCE STARTUP VANTA VALUED AT $4 BILLION IN NEW FUNDING ROUND
articles.summary: Vanta's valuation surged to $4 billion after closing a $150 million funding round backed by CrowdStrike Ventures. The startup, which helps companies manage compliance with cybersecurity frameworks like SOC 2 and ISO 27001, serves over 12,000 customers, including tech giants Atlassian and Snowflake. More investors included Wellington Management, Atlassian Ventures, JPMorgan Chase, and Sequoia Capital.

articles.title: HOW TO GET RECOMMENDED BY CHATGPT
articles.summary: AI answer engines are becoming the most influential gatekeepers in B2B buying, with ChatGPT hitting a billion users and 2.5 billion daily prompts. Buyers now ask AI what to buy instead of researching themselves - if your product isn't mentioned, it might as well not exist. Josh Blyskal from Profound found only 11% of citations overlap between ChatGPT and Perplexity, meaning you need platform-specific strategies. Reddit is now the most-cited domain at 6% of citations, and one-in-three citations come from comparative listicles. Almost half of cited domains shift within a single month.

articles.title: YOUR COMPLETE GUIDE TO REVENUE LEAKAGE
articles.summary: Revenue leakage is the gap between what you should earn and what you actually collect - the ghost quietly draining dollars through missed invoices, zombie accounts still getting service, and pilot pricing that never graduates. It's a systems problem disguised as a people problem, living in the cracks between sales handoffs and billing processes. Common culprits include auto-renewals that silently fail, discounts hardcoded forever, and churned customers still accessing your product months later.

articles.title: REFRAMING AI CHATBOTS AS SELF-SERVICE TOOLS
articles.summary: AI chatbots should be reframed as self-service tools rather than human support replacements due to their limitations. Achieving this involves clearly defining their purpose and implementing technical guardrails to ensure alignment. Understanding AI's capabilities and limits helps in effectively integrating them into customer support.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF SAAS PRICING, FEATURING KYLE POYAR
articles.summary: This episode of Metronome's _Future of SaaS Pricing_ features Kyle Poyar, Co-Founder & Operating Partner at Tremont. Kyle shares 2025 pricing trends [9] and how to stay competitive in the rapidly evolving

articles.title: THE SUBSTACK AI REPORT (RESOURCE)
articles.summary: Substack surveyed 2,000 publishers and found 45% use AI - mostly men over 45 in tech/business categories who rely on it for research and editing, not generating full posts.

articles.title: AI MEETING NOTES BY NOTION
articles.summary: AI Meeting Notes by Notion is a tool that transcribes and organizes meeting notes directly within Notion, synced with your calendar.

articles.title: PRDKIT
articles.summary: PRDKit is an AI tool that generates structured product specs, wireframes, and go-to-market assets from feature ideas.

articles.title: ZERO-SUM THINKING AND THE LABOR MARKET
articles.summary: College graduates now have higher unemployment rates than the overall workforce for the first time since 2018 - the opposite of how this is supposed to work. Young men are struggling most, with 50,000 healthcare jobs going to women graduates while men gained far fewer positions across all sectors. People are applying to 1,000 jobs and getting rejected by algorithms in what's become a casino economy where getting hired feels like winning the lottery. This broken system feeds zero-sum thinking - the belief that my success requires your failure - which has replaced the Sputnik generation's positive-sum worldview where everyone could grow together.

articles.title: THE GENERAL THEORY OF ENSHITTIFICATION
articles.summary: Enshittification isn't just about social media - it's the natural lifecycle of any business with network effects. Companies offer great value to build their user base, then exploit that base once locked in. Uber's "earnings inflection strategy" jumping from losses to profits by squeezing customers proves this was always the plan.

articles.title: WHAT FOUNDERS TEACH US
articles.summary: Founders are river people, not villagers - they thrive in uncertainty while others choose stability. They see differently: they're fearless enough to hear "no" constantly but keep asking, they actively seek contrarians to challenge their thinking, and they know execution beats strategy. They ignore the right feedback, focus obsessively on recruiting A-players, and carry unshakable optimism despite knowing 90% of startups fail.

articles.title: PAGERANK IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: The internet's content distribution is about to mirror online advertising's auction system, but without the ads.

articles.title: YOU NEED TO HAVE A POINT OF VIEW
articles.summary: Rising executives often freeze when asked "What should we do?" because they're afraid of being wrong.

articles.title: BEHIND-THE-SCENES WITH ONE OF THE FASTEST GROWING WELLNESS STARTUPS
articles.summary: Fay built a platform that connects tens of thousands of clients with dietitians covered by insurance and raised $75M from Goldman Sachs and General Catalyst.
